key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2024 excess revenues over expenses $2,134,121 / 141,666 shares (2025-12-31 annual); excludes FY2025 one-time land sales and Dry Lake cattle charges
Groundbreaker base: $9.9M midpoint steady-state lease+royalty by 2029; 16x contracted-renewable comp multiple

open_questions: Confirm stapled Aztec Land Company, LLC unit economics and whether look-through should include Company-level leases.
Review related-party transactions (Page, Railway, consulting) for minority-holder fairness.
Model water adjudication timeline and enforceable AF entitlement (`nav_overlay.not_in_model_requires_refresh`).
CIK remains null; OTC download path added to `us_ticker_config.json` and `download_otc_api.py`.

Trusted (Groundbreaker RE, approved 2026-06-02):** $1.3M minimum operational rent floor, project-level royalty ramp ($6.8–12.9M steady state), per-acre marks, and renewable lease schedules in Lawrence growth and segment build.
thesis: Aztec Land and Cattle Company, Limited is a 140-year-old Arizona land lessor incorporated in 1885 and re-domesticated to Delaware in 2023. **[Fact]** The corporation and related entities own roughly 239,000 acres of surface land and 318,000 acres of mineral rights in Navajo and Coconino Counties (`investor-documents/ir-azlcz/2025-12-31_Annual_Report.pdf`, Note 1). **[Fact]** Revenue comes mainly from renewable energy ground leases (solar and wind), grazing leases, access agreements, and related farm operations; the company also holds a 77.5% economic interest in the Apache Railway short line through The Apache Railroad Company, LLC. **[Fact]** Shares trade OTC Pink (AZLCZ) with thin volume; the company is not an SEC registrant and publishes audited tax-basis annual reports via OTC Markets. **[Fact]** At December 31, 2025 the company had 141,666 shares outstanding and tax-basis stockholders' equity of $7.76 million (~$55 per share).
why_market_wrong: The stock may look like a tiny book-value oddity while embedding a large, hard-to-replicate land and infrastructure bundle in northeast Arizona. **[Inference]** Tax-basis financials carry land at roughly $13 per acre and assign no balance-sheet value to mineral rights transferred to Aztec Minerals, LLC, so accounting equity understates economic assets (TPL-style GAAP misstatement pattern). **[Inference]** Renewable lease income has become the majority of cash collections, and one solar project entered a 30-year operations term in 2025, yet the market may still price AZLCZ as a legacy ranch rather than a phased renewable landlord. **[Inference]** OTC Pink listing, stapled LLC units, related-party financing, and 40-year Little Colorado water adjudication keep institutional screens away, sustaining a market-structure discount even as the share price has risen sharply.
